# Open Magi Agent

Open-source Magi Agent runtime for local CLI and dashboard work.

## Canonical Links

- Website: https://openmagi.ai
- Source: https://github.com/openmagi/magi-agent
- Docs: https://openmagi.ai/docs
- Full agent docs: https://openmagi.ai/docs/llms-full.txt

## Install

```bash
brew install --force-bottle openmagi/tap/magi-agent
magi-agent serve --port 8080
open http://localhost:8080/dashboard
```

Source checkouts are for contributors. Normal local use is Homebrew plus one
provider key, then `magi` for CLI work or `magi-agent serve` for the dashboard.

## Docs Index

- Open Magi Docs: https://openmagi.ai/docs - Open-source local runtime docs.
- Getting Started: https://openmagi.ai/docs/getting-started - Homebrew install plus provider-key setup.
- Quickstart: https://openmagi.ai/docs/quickstart - Run a first `magi -p` task.
- CLI: https://openmagi.ai/docs/cli - `magi` and `magi-agent serve` usage.
- Configuration: https://openmagi.ai/docs/configuration - Provider keys, models, local config, and optional integrations.
- Architecture: https://openmagi.ai/docs/architecture - Runtime state, execution contracts, memory, and evidence gates.
- Changelog: https://openmagi.ai/docs/changelog - User-facing release notes.

## Core Concepts

- Local runtime installed by Homebrew, with both `magi` and `magi-agent`.
- Provider-neutral model routing across Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and Fireworks.
- First-party local tools are gated by permission mode.
- Evidence, hooks, harnesses, memory, and projection make runtime behavior auditable.
